work_unit: SZ-SP-M-003: HistoryTab refresh() stale closure — no mounted check, double-fetch in StrictMode
situation: Derived fix from audit-2026-07-07-047. HistoryTab.tsx defines refresh() inside component body and calls it in useEffect with empty deps. In React StrictMode (dev), useEffect fires mount→unmount→remount, causing two concurrent getHistory IPC calls. If component unmounts before response arrives, setHistory/setLoading fire on unmounted component (React warning + potential memory leak). Prior SZ-SP-M-002 fix added .catch/.finally but did not add mounted guard.
action: Added useRef(true) mountedRef to HistoryTab. useEffect sets mountedRef.current=true on mount, returns cleanup setting it false. All three async callbacks (.then/.catch/.finally) early-return if !mountedRef.current before calling setState. Reused existing opt-sidepanel-1782907225 worktree (route-fix same area). cp-guard triggered (diverged wt), used case-104 escape hatch: direct edit + commit inside worktree.
outcome_evidence: Commit d3010ed on auto/opt-sidepanel-1782907225 pushed to origin. Diff: +10/-2 lines adding useRef import, mountedRef declaration, 3x mounted guard checks in refresh() callbacks, useEffect cleanup return. Build not run (pure React lifecycle fix, no API change).
next_suggestion: Next pending fix: SZ-SP-M-004 (Progress listener race condition — interleaved events from concurrent analyses). Same sidepanel area, can reuse this worktree.

audit_findings.finding: useEffect calls refresh() without mounted guard. StrictMode double-invokes effect causing duplicate IPC. Async callbacks call setState after unmount.
audit_findings.remediation: Add useRef mounted flag, check in all async callbacks, cleanup on unmount.
